text:
No single confirmed beachhead — this is explicitly an OPEN QUESTION in the docs (bets-and-open-questions.md Q10): "The brainstorm leaned 'solo dev running Claude Code + Codex.' But the todo/podcast example suggests the richer beachhead might be 'the individual who already has fabric-capable agents across several apps.' ... Unresolved."
 [...] - The beachhead user is an explicitly OPEN, unresolved question (Q10) — don't let landing-page copy assert a confident "built for X" persona the docs themselves haven't settled. [...] No single confirmed beachhead — this is explicitly an OPEN QUESTION in the docs (bets-and-open-questions.md Q10): "The brainstorm leaned 'solo dev running Claude Code + Codex.' But the todo/podcast example suggests the richer beachhead might be 'the individual who already has fabric-capable agents across several apps.' ... Unresolved."

What IS grounded/shipped: the actual acute pain (problem-space.md) is pains #1–3 — human-as-glue tax, amnesiac/non-portable agents, app silos — explicitly marked **acute** (felt daily, today) vs. pains #6–8 (push-not-pull, cross-person coordination, provenance) marked **aspirational**. Pain #5 ("multiple agents, zero shared awareness" — the dev-fleet collision story) is explicitly flagged: "the pain the dev-fleet framing over-indexes on. It's real but narrow" and "the seductive demo we must not let define the project" (problem-space.md:82,85).

So: the honest target user today is a **solo developer running multiple coding-agent hosts (Claude Code + Codex + …) who wants durable identity and shared awareness across sessions/devices** — but the docs themselves warn against over-indexing on the "agents collide on the same file" framing as the pitch, since that's unproven (see Bets Q1) and explicitly demoted to "ceiling, not floor" in value-layers.md. [...] Lead with the grounded floor: "Citizenship for your agents" + host-neutral durable identity + cross-host/cross-device awareness for your own fleet (Product A, Rung 0–1) — this is real, tested, and matches tenet #9 (no dashboard) and #3 (single-player value day one). Use the vision/society material (multi-app agents, coordination, cross-person) as clearly-labeled "where this is headed," not as present-tense claims — the docs' own authors were careful to mark that boundary and copy should preserve it. [...] - The beachhead user is an explicitly OPEN, unresolved question (Q10) — don't let landing-page copy assert a confident "built for X" persona the docs themselves haven't settled.
